description_long: This object is an irregularly shaped, dark brown stone with a roughly triangular profile and a sharp, thin edge along one side. Its surface is matte with subtle undulations and a few visible facets, suggesting it may have been chipped or fractured naturally or intentionally. The stone is relatively flat when viewed from the side, with a thickness much less than its height or width, giving it a blade-like appearance. The coloration is predominantly a deep, earthy brown with some lighter and darker mottling, but no distinct veining or artificial markings. The edges are uneven and jagged, with one side appearing slightly more pointed and the opposite side broader and more blunt. This stone could easily be found as a loose obstacle on a sidewalk, in a park, or along a garden path in an urban setting.
description: A dark brown, irregularly shaped stone with a thin, blade-like profile and jagged edges. Its surface is matte and subtly mottled, with no distinct patterns.
description_view_0: The stone appears broad and roughly triangular, with a jagged top and a wide, uneven base.
description_view_1: From the side, the stone is very thin and blade-like, emphasizing its sharp edge and flatness.
description_view_2: This view shows a similar broad triangular shape as view 0, but with slightly different edge contours and surface undulations.
description_view_3: The stone again appears thin and elongated, with the opposite edge from view 1 visible, showing a slightly more rounded profile.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or loose obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle mottling with no distinct veining or artificial carvings; surface is irregular but not patterned.
attribute_weathering: Surface appears matte and slightly rough, suggesting moderate natural weathering but no heavy erosion or lichen growth.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically contrasts with smoother, man-made surfaces like pavement but may blend with natural soil or gravel; not embedded or covered by vegetation.
